subtopics.name: Algebra & Partial Fractions
learning_outcomes: Understand the meaning of |x|, sketch the graph of y=|ax+b|, and solve related equations and inequalities [cite: 6, 14]
Divide a polynomial (degree ≤ 4) by a linear or quadratic polynomial to identify the quotient and remainder [cite: 7, 15]
Apply the factor theorem and the remainder theorem to solve polynomial equations or evaluate coefficients [cite: 8, 15, 16]
Decompose rational functions into partial fractions for denominators with linear factors, repeated linear factors, or a quadratic factor [cite: 9, 10]
Use the expansion of (1+x)^n where n is a rational number and |x|<1, including adapting standard series [cite: 11, 19]

subtopics.name: Logarithmic & Exponential Functions
learning_outcomes: Use the laws of logarithms and understand the relationship between logarithms and indices [cite: 26]
Understand the definitions, properties, and graphs of e^x and ln x, including their relationship as inverse functions [cite: 27, 31]
Solve equations and inequalities in which the unknown appears in indices [cite: 28, 32]
Use logarithms to transform non-linear relationships to linear form to determine unknown constants [cite: 29, 34, 35]

subtopics.name: Trigonometry
learning_outcomes: Use the secant, cosecant, and cotangent functions, including their relationships to primary functions and their graphs [cite: 38, 39]
Apply trigonometric identities involving sec^2 θ, cosec^2 θ, and compound/addition angle formulae [cite: 40, 41, 42, 43]
Use double-angle formulae for sin 2A, cos 2A, and tan 2A [cite: 44]
Express a sin θ + b cos θ in the forms R sin(θ ± α) or R cos(θ ± α) to solve equations [cite: 45, 48]

subtopics.name: Differentiation
learning_outcomes: Use the derivatives of e^x, ln x, sin x, cos x, tan x, and tan^-1 x [cite: 55]
Apply the product and quotient rules to find derivatives [cite: 56, 60]
Find and use the first derivative of a function defined parametrically or implicitly [cite: 57, 61, 62]
Solve problems involving tangents and normals using various differentiation techniques [cite: 63]

subtopics.name: Integration
learning_outcomes: Integrate functions involving e^(ax+b), 1/(ax+b), sin(ax+b), cos(ax+b), sec^2(ax+b), and 1/(x^2+a^2) [cite: 66, 77]
Use trigonometric relationships and double-angle formulae to carry out integration [cite: 67, 78]
Integrate rational functions via decomposition into partial fractions [cite: 68, 69, 79]
Recognize and integrate functions of the form kf'(x)/f(x) [cite: 70, 72, 80]
Apply integration by parts and use given substitutions to simplify and evaluate integrals [cite: 74, 75, 82, 83]

subtopics.name: Numerical Solutions
learning_outcomes: Locate a root approximately using graphical considerations or sign changes [cite: 92, 96]
Understand the notation for a sequence of approximations and the possibility of iteration failure [cite: 93, 97]
Use a given iteration formula x_n+1 = F(x_n) to determine a root to a prescribed degree of accuracy [cite: 94]

subtopics.name: Vectors
learning_outcomes: Use standard vector notations and carry out addition, subtraction, and scalar multiplication [cite: 99]
Calculate the magnitude of a vector and use unit, displacement, and position vectors [cite: 99]
Find and use the equation of a straight line in the form r = a + tb [cite: 99]
Determine if two lines are parallel, intersect, or are skew, and find points of intersection [cite: 99]
Calculate and use the scalar product to solve problems involving angles and perpendiculars [cite: 99]

subtopics.name: Differential Equations
learning_outcomes: Formulate simple statements involving a rate of change as a differential equation [cite: 106, 121]
Solve first-order differential equations using the separation of variables [cite: 107, 122]
Find particular solutions using initial conditions and interpret solutions in a modeling context [cite: 108, 109, 123]

subtopics.name: Complex Numbers
learning_outcomes: Understand Cartesian form x + iy, including modulus, argument, and the conjugate of a complex number [cite: 112, 113, 125]
Use the conjugate pair result for polynomial equations with real coefficients [cite: 114, 128]
Represent complex numbers on an Argand diagram and understand the geometrical effects of operations [cite: 115, 118]
Carry out multiplication and division in polar form r(cos θ + i sin θ) or exponential form re^iθ [cite: 116, 129, 130]
Find square roots of a complex number and illustrate loci defined by equations or inequalities [cite: 117, 119, 131, 132]

subtopics.name: Poisson Distribution
learning_outcomes: Calculate probabilities for the Poisson distribution Po(λ) where mean and variance equal λ [cite: 141, 142]
Understand the relevance of the Poisson distribution to random events and use it as a model [cite: 143, 144]
Apply the Poisson distribution as an approximation to the Binomial distribution [cite: 145, 149]
Use the Normal distribution with a continuity correction to approximate the Poisson distribution [cite: 146, 150]

subtopics.name: Linear Combinations of Random Variables
learning_outcomes: Use results for expectation and variance of aX + b and independent aX + bY [cite: 152, 153, 154, 155]
Understand the distribution of linear combinations of independent Normal and Poisson variables [cite: 156, 157, 158]

subtopics.name: Continuous Random Variables
learning_outcomes: Understand the concept of a continuous random variable and the properties of a probability density function [cite: 167]
Use a density function to solve problems involving probabilities, mean, and variance [cite: 168, 171]
Locate the median and other percentiles using the density function [cite: 173]

subtopics.name: Sampling & Estimation
learning_outcomes: Distinguish between a sample and population and recognize the need for random sampling [cite: 178, 188]
Understand the distribution of the sample mean and apply the Central Limit Theorem for large samples [cite: 180, 181, 183, 191]
Calculate unbiased estimates of population mean and variance from raw or summarized data [cite: 184, 192, 193]
Determine and interpret confidence intervals for a population mean or proportion [cite: 185, 186]

subtopics.name: Hypothesis Testing
learning_outcomes: Understand null/alternative hypotheses, one-tailed/two-tailed tests, significance levels, and rejection regions [cite: 201, 209]
Carry out hypothesis tests for a single observation from a Binomial or Poisson distribution [cite: 202, 203, 204]
Carry out hypothesis tests concerning the population mean for Normal distributions or large samples [cite: 205]
Understand and calculate the probabilities of making Type I and Type II errors [cite: 206, 207]
